description_short: New Orleans sits below sea level on the Gulf Coast, making it one of the most hurricane- and flood-vulnerable major cities in North America.
description_long: Located between the Mississippi River and Lake Pontchartrain, much of New Orleans is below sea level and protected by a federal levee system. The city faces severe hurricane and storm-surge risk from June through November, plus year-round severe weather including tornadoes during the spring and fall. Orleans Parish is served by NWS New Orleans/Baton Rouge (office code LIX) within forecast zone LAZ077 (Orleans).
